transform.calculate: if(datum.type === 'Strongly disagree',-2,0) + if(datum.type==='Disagree',-1,0) + if(datum.type =='Neither agree nor disagree',0,0) + if(datum.type ==='Agree',1,0) + if(datum.type ==='Strongly agree',2,0)
if(datum.type === 'Disagree' || datum.type === 'Strongly disagree', datum.percentage,0) + if(datum.type === 'Neither agree nor disagree', datum.percentage / 2,0)

circle_github_punchcard.description: Punchcard Visualization like on Github. The day on y-axis uses a custom order from Monday to Sunday.  The sort property supports both full day names (e.g., 'Monday') and their three letter initials (e.g., 'mon') -- both of which are case insensitive.

histogram_rel_freq.description: Relative frequency histogram. The data is binned with first transform. The number of values per bin and the total number are calculated in the second and third transform to calculate the relative frequency in the last transformation step.

layer.description: transparent layer to make it easier to trigger selection

text: Population Distribution by Socioeconomic Level in Fictional Cities

transform.calculate: datum['high_percent'] > 0.8 ? '#FF0F0F' : datum['medium_percent'] > 0.8 ? '#FFC333' : datum['low_percent'] > 0.8 ? '#32F95D' : datum['high_percent'] > 0.5 && datum['medium_percent'] > datum['low_percent'] ? '#ff3c18' : datum['high_percent'] > 0.5 && datum['medium_percent'] < datum['low_percent'] ? '#cb4922' : datum['medium_percent'] > 0.5 && datum['high_percent'] > datum['low_percent'] ? '#ff962a' : datum['medium_percent'] > 0.5 && datum['high_percent'] < datum['low_percent'] ? '#cbd03d' : datum['low_percent'] > 0.5 && datum['medium_percent'] < datum['high_percent'] ? '#65be49' : datum['low_percent'] > 0.5 && datum['medium_percent'] > datum['high_percent'] ? '#65eb52' : '#43c1ce'

trellis_bar_no_header.description: A trellis bar chart showing the US population distribution of age groups and gender in 2000.

trellis_barley.name: trellis_barley
trellis_barley.description: The Trellis display by Becker et al. helped establish small multiples as a “powerful mechanism for understanding interactions in studies of how a response depends on explanatory variables”. Here we reproduce a trellis of Barley yields from the 1930s, complete with main-effects ordering to facilitate comparison.

trellis_barley_independent.name: trellis_barley
trellis_barley_independent.description: The Trellis display by Becker et al. helped establish small multiples as a “powerful mechanism for understanding interactions in studies of how a response depends on explanatory variables”. Here we reproduce a trellis of Barley yields from the 1930s, complete with main-effects ordering to facilitate comparison.

trellis_barley_layer_median.name: trellis_barley
trellis_barley_layer_median.description: The Trellis display by Becker et al. helped establish small multiples as a “powerful mechanism for understanding interactions in studies of how a response depends on explanatory variables”. Here we reproduce a trellis of Barley yields from the 1930s, complete with main-effects ordering to facilitate comparison.

trellis_line_quarter.description: Stock price mean per quarter broken down by years.

trellis_scatter_binned_row.description: A trellis scatterplot showing Horsepower and Miles per gallons, faceted by binned values of Acceleration.

vconcat_weather.description: Two vertically concatenated charts that show a histogram of precipitation in Seattle and the relationship between min and max temperature.

window_cumulative_running_average.description: A line graph showing how the overall average Miles Per Gallon for how the MPG has changed over the years. The red line shows the average, and is layered on top of all the raw data.

window_impute_null.description: Using window transform to impute missing values in a line chart by averaging the previous and next values.

window_top_k.description: A bar graph showing the scores of the top 5 students. This shows an example of the window transform, for how the top K (5) can be filtered, and also how a rank can be computed for each student.

window_top_k_others.description: Top-K plot with "others" by Trevor Manz, adapted from https://observablehq.com/collection/@manzt/vega-vega-lite-examples.
window_top_k_others.title: Top Directors by Average Worldwide Gross
